title: ssh client known_hosts handling outstanding work
body:
- [ ] Interactively prompt for known_hosts inclusion before adding an entry
- [ ] Either make handling of missing known_hosts and known_hosts that miss an entry _for a specific machine_ consistent, or justify the differences
- [ ] Document the rationale for any compromises / deviations from the basic expectation that all ssh connections are secure, except for cases where the user makes an intentional decision to the contrary. That includes the precise semantics of `InsecureIsMachineConnection`.
- [ ] Examine how `connection_golang.go` and `connection_native.go` are / are not consistent (e.g. WRT interactive prompting methods and file descriptors used, or `InsecureIsMachineConnection`, if it becomes applicable to the native mode)
- [ ] Look into replacing `InsecureIsMachineConnection` with provisioning private keys into the machine, and using public keys to create the client connection.

comments.body:
I've just run into an issue with `known_hosts` in Podman while setting up podman-remote to access an Alpine Linux host.

The issue boiled down to the fact that I had an SSH client config from http://ssh-audit.com that told `ssh` to not use `ecdsa-sha2-nistp256`. `ssh` would add both the RSA and ED25519 keys to known_hosts, then when I tried to connect with `podman-remote`, I would get a known hosts failure because of a mismatching key—podman seemingly was only looking for the `ecdsa-sha2-nistp256` key, despite the two others being available.

I could solve this either by:

- removing the restrictive config for `ssh` so that the `ecdsa-sha2-nistp256` key was added to `known_hosts` and then picked up by podman-remote
- adding a more restrictive `sshd_config` on the server so that `ecdsa-sha2-nistp256` wouldn't be given as an option, forcing podman-remote to select ed25519 instead

The root cause was my custom ssh config, but ideally podman-remote would be able to negotiate and use the ed25519 key instead.

Looks like @willhbr 's issue might have been solved by https://github.com/containers/common/pull/2212.

Looks like that has not yet been released. It was pulled into Podman on commit f6af35c695f2a41360629da041cd26a6dca401e9 which is only in the very recent Podman v5.4.0-rc1 release:

```
$ git tag --contains f6af35c695f2a41360629da041cd26a6dca401e9
v5.4.0-rc1
```

https://github.com/containers/podman/releases/tag/v5.4.0-rc1
